full_response:
Work

1.  Choose King Arthur’s inertial frame 𝒦.  
    •  The starting line is at the origin O : (t_K , x_K ) = (0 , 0).  
    •  The finish line is fixed a distance L down‐track at x_K = L and is at rest in this frame.  

2.  World-line of either knight in Arthur’s frame  
    (the race is run at constant speed v as specified in the set-up):

        x_K (t_K) = v t_K .                                (1)

3.  Coordinates of the finish event E_F.  
    At E_F the position coordinate equals that of the finish line, x_K = L.  
    Insert this in (1):

        L = v t_K    ⟹   t_K = L / v.                      (2)

4.  Therefore

        x_K(E_F) = L ,    t_K(E_F) = L / v.               (3)

Answer
\boxed{(t_K,\;x_K)_{E_F}=\left(\dfrac{L}{v},\,L\right)}

Confidence: 50%
